title: Skill template: packages/cli/templates/skills/fabric-review/SKILL.md
description: Author the fabric-review Skill template that drives the AI-side review loop. Skill embeds: (a) mode inference rules — recent user message scan + events.jsonl tail check + pending count → infer one of {pending, topic, health, revisit} mode; (b) per-mode flow blocks describing what to do once mode is inferred; (c) semantic check guidance for [b] (LLM-assisted duplicate/contradiction detection across pending+canonical entries) — note: the planning prompt mentioned [b] mode but the locked mode list is {pending,topic,health,revisit}, so semantic check guidance attaches to the 'topic' mode; (d) AskUserQuestion usage rules — only for genuine choices (approve/reject/modify per item), NEVER for mode picking.

files.change: Write a complete SKILL.md with frontmatter (name:fabric-review, description, tools:[fab_review,Read,Bash]) followed by structured sections: Mode Inference, Per-Mode Flow (4 modes), Semantic Check Guidance (under topic mode), Choice Surface Rules (when to AskUserQuestion vs not). Mirror the prose-as-checklist style of templates/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md.

implementation: Read packages/cli/templates/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md to absorb the existing tone, frontmatter shape, section-as-prose convention, and AskUserQuestion handling guidance — fabric-review SKILL.md must feel like a sibling document

Write frontmatter: ---
name: fabric-review
description: Review pending knowledge entries — list, approve, reject, modify (incl. layer flip), search, defer. Mode inferred from invocation context.
tools:
  - mcp__fabric__fab_review
  - Read
  - Bash
--- (mirror fabric-archive frontmatter structure)

Author 'Mode Inference' section: enumerate the 4 modes (pending/topic/health/revisit) with their semantic distinctions; provide a 3-step inference algorithm for the AI to follow (Step 1 scan recent user message for keywords like 'review', 'approve', 'about <topic>', 'all clean?', 'last week'; Step 2 check events.jsonl tail for last knowledge_proposed cluster; Step 3 if user message is generic, default to mode inferred from pending count: >=10 pending → 'pending' mode; <10 + recent layer_changed events → 'health' mode; otherwise → 'pending'). Explicit anti-pattern: 'NEVER call AskUserQuestion to ask the user which mode to use — the system MUST infer.'

Author 'Per-Mode Flow' section with one subsection per mode: (1) pending mode: call fab_review action='list'; for each item present a per-item AskUserQuestion with options [approve, reject, modify, defer, skip]; route choice to corresponding fab_review action. (2) topic mode: extract topic keyword from user message; call fab_review action='search' with query=topic; perform semantic check (see Semantic Check Guidance); present findings to user; offer AskUserQuestion for any flagged dups/contradictions. (3) health mode: call fab_review list + search to gather corpus stats (counts by type/layer/maturity, oldest pending age, layer_changed count from events.jsonl); render a status summary; only AskUserQuestion if user wants to act on flagged orphans (defer overdue, etc). (4) revisit mode: read events.jsonl from N days ago; surface entries that were approved/rejected/modified in that window; offer to re-review.

Author 'Semantic Check Guidance' subsection (under topic mode): describe how the AI should compare candidate pending entries vs existing canonical entries — heuristics: (a) title keyword overlap > 60% suggests near-duplicate, recommend modify or reject; (b) summary contradicts existing decisions/<id>--<slug>.md (e.g. 'use deepMerge' vs 'avoid deepMerge') flag as contradiction; (c) pitfall pending that already has matching guideline → flag as redundant; (d) tags fully subsume an existing entry → suggest merge

Author 'AskUserQuestion Rules' section: explicit DO list (approve/reject/modify/defer per item; merge resolution; layer flip yes/no) and explicit DON'T list (mode picking; whether to invoke skill at all; reading frontmatter — that's deterministic). Provide example phrasing for the per-item prompt: header='Review pending entry', question='What action for {title}?', options [approve, reject, modify, defer, skip].

Author 'Frontmatter Modify Conventions' section: layer-flip is the only legal stable_id mutation; modifying maturity/tags/title/summary preserves id; modifying layer triggers fab_review modify with changes.layer (server handles id reallocation + git mv). Reference Q1 filename convention: <id>--<slug>.md format.
Author 'Output Contract' section: skill must always produce a brief summary (entries reviewed, actions taken, ledger events emitted). Surface git status diff and recent events.jsonl tail to the user.
Add validation block at the top of file that the skill should run ('Preconditions': .fabric/ exists, fab_review tool available, agents.meta.json present)
Verify file size 4-8 KB (matches fabric-archive SKILL.md sizing)
Commit as 'feat(cli): add fabric-review skill template with mode inference (rc.3 TASK-003)'

reference.pattern: Skill template prose-checklist style — fabric-archive SKILL.md is the sibling reference
reference.examples: Mirror fabric-archive: frontmatter → preconditions → main flow → AskUserQuestion rules → output contract

criteria: SKILL.md exists at packages/cli/templates/skills/fabric-review/SKILL.md with valid YAML frontmatter (parseable by gray-matter or equivalent)
File contains exactly 4 mode flow subsections labeled pending/topic/health/revisit — verified by grep '^## Mode:' returning 4 matches
File contains 'Mode Inference' section with explicit 3-step algorithm and 'NEVER ask the user which mode' anti-pattern statement — verified by substring match
File contains 'Semantic Check Guidance' subsection mentioning duplicate/contradiction detection — verified by substring match
File contains 'AskUserQuestion Rules' section with explicit DO and DON'T lists — verified by substring match
All 4 fab_review actions referenced (list/approve/reject/modify/search/defer all called out in at least one mode flow)
File size between 3KB and 12KB (matching fabric-archive sizing); flag if outside range

rationale.chosen_approach: Prose-checklist SKILL.md mirroring fabric-archive sibling; 4 mode flow blocks + mode inference algorithm + semantic check guidance + AskUserQuestion rules
alternatives_considered: Single mode without inference — rejected: Q-review locked the 4-mode design
Mode picker via AskUserQuestion — rejected explicitly by Q-review (system must infer)
Separate fabric-review-list / fabric-review-approve skills — rejected: Cursor's skill discovery + 40-tool cap favor unified entry point
decision_factors: Mode inference is a Locked-In Q-review decision
AskUserQuestion scope is locked to genuine choices
Semantic check is delegated to the LLM (not server-side) per Boundary B
rationale.tradeoffs: Prose-as-program limits machine-checkability; mitigated by explicit grep-verifiable section headers in convergence criteria

manual_checks: Open SKILL.md in Claude Code session; invoke `fabric-review` skill on a fixture with 5 pending entries; verify mode inference selects correctly without prompting user
Verify per-item AskUserQuestion fires for each pending entry
success_metrics: All convergence criteria substring matches return >=1
File installed correctly in TASK-006 verification

risks.description: Skill prose too vague — AI mis-infers mode in real session
risks.mitigation: Include explicit examples in the inference algorithm (sample user messages → expected mode); dogfood test (TASK-008) is the empirical check
risks.fallback: Iterate prompt in rc.3.1 if dogfood reveals systematic mis-inference
